channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: CIO100 2024: The top transformational tech leaders and teams in Southeast Asia and Hong Kong
item.description: CIO ASEAN is proud to announce the winners of the 2024 CIO100 Awards – recognising the top technology leaders and teams across Southeast Asia and Hong Kong who are driving innovation and influencing rapid change.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why the crypto community loves Howard Lutnick, Trump’s pick for Commerce secretary
item.description: Donald Trump’s selection on Tuesday of Howard Lutnick as Commerce secretary brought cheers from the cryptocurrency sector. Little surprise there. Lutnick has long advocated for cryptocurrency to be treated on equal footing with other financial assets.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft brings automated ‘agents’ to M365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new tool in Microsoft 365 Copilot to automate repetitive tasks, part of a drive to make the generative AI (genAI) assistant more useful to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: A New Hampshire company is working to develop a fleet of autonomous helicopters that it hopes can be used to put out fires, spray crops and handle other dangerous jobs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational
item.description: The ETAC has quickly scaled from a pilot project to an operational center at the Energy Department for analyzing cyber threats like Volt Typhoon. The post Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: El Capitan bumps Frontier to claim world’s fastest supercomputer title
item.description: The new El Capitan system at the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California made its debut at No. 1 on the latest TOP500 list of the world’s most powerful supercomputers, released today. The Frontier system at Oak Ridge National Laboratory in Tennessee moved down to the No.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Bay Area Wins Computing Crown with El Capitan Supercomputer
item.description: The Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory's new supercomputer, El Capitan, has been deemed the world's fastest by the TOP500 list and a symbol of the US commitment to innovation in high-performance computing technology.
item.dc:creator: Lisa M. Krieger, The Mercury News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built
item.description: A new challenge looks to digitally recreate the planning of each module of the International Space Station. The post A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: For November, Patch Tuesday includes three Windows zero-day fixes
item.description: Microsoft’s November Patch Tuesday release addresses 89 vulnerabilities in Windows, SQL Server, .NET and Microsoft Office — and three zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43451, CVE-2024-49019 and CVE-2024-49039) that mean a patch now recommendation for Windows platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity
item.description: If left unchecked, Microsoft’s practices will become the industry standard, clearing the path for other providers to harm customers and weaken their defenses. The post Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Rust Foundation moves forward on C++ and Rust interoperability
item.description: The Rust Foundation, which stewards development of the Rust language, has released a statement addressing challenges and opportunities for interoperability between Rust and C++, with the intent of making cross-language development more accessible and approachable.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to use DispatchProxy for AOP in .NET Core
item.description: Aspect-oriented programming (AOP) decomposes an application’s source code into distinct aspects that isolate the core business logic from cross-cutting concerns.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple announces new feature to help airlines track your lost luggage
item.description: For air travelers who have popped an AirTag in their luggage – but still struggle to get help when suitcases are misplaced – this software update is for you.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation
